symptoms.character: fresh red blood with mucus, roughly every 30 minutes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: >6 bloody stools/day is the stool-frequency limb of Truelove and Witts severe disease.
Bloody diarrhoea is also consistent with an infective trigger, which must be excluded on admission.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe crampy lower-abdominal pain with tenesmus
symptoms.character: unbearable cramp before defecation then a constant unrelieved painful urge

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tenesmus and cramping reflect active distal colonic/rectal inflammation typical of UC.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fever with malaise and profound weakness (systemic toxicity)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fever is a systemic-toxicity limb of Truelove and Witts severe disease and raises toxic-megacolon risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Postural dizziness / near-syncope with palpitations, unable to stand

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Patient-reportable proxy for orthostatic hypotension and impending hypovolaemic shock.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unintentional weight loss ~4 kg in one week

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid loss reflects fluid depletion, protein-losing enteropathy and catabolism in severe disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cessation of maintenance mesalazine (unable to swallow due to nausea)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Loss of anti-inflammatory maintenance can worsen a flare; inability to swallow also signals oral therapy is no longer viable.

symptoms.symptom_name: Nausea with inability to tolerate oral intake

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inability to rehydrate orally accelerates volume depletion and mandates IV resuscitation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known 4-year history of ulcerative colitis (established baseline)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Established UC is the substrate; the acute severe flare is the diagnosis of the encounter.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypotension and tachycardia (BP 95/60, HR 118) with orthostatic change

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective distributive/hypovolaemic compromise — sealed clinician measurement; the AI Doctor infers this from the reported near-syncope, not a measured BP.
Confirms the systemic-toxicity criterion; the AI has only the patient-reported home thermometer value, not a clinician measurement.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diffuse lower-abdominal tenderness with sluggish bowel sounds, no board-like peritonism

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Serial abdominal examination is central to detecting evolving toxic megacolon/perforation and cannot be done in telehealth.
Clinical anaemia is a Truelove and Witts severity limb; degree of anaemia needs a blood test.

symptoms.symptom_name: Full blood count / CRP / electrolytes / lactate (severity and resuscitation markers)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Venous bloods — FBC, CRP, U&E, LFT, lactate, group and hold
investigation_details.expected_result: Anaemia, raised inflammatory markers, electrolyte disturbance and hypovolaemic markers expected (values determined in hospital, not telehealth)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Severity, anaemia and electrolyte derangement go unquantified; resuscitation cannot be guided.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective severity markers — hospital investigation, never available in real time in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Stool Clostridioides difficile testing / stool culture

investigation_details.investigation_name: Stool C. difficile toxin/PCR and culture
investigation_details.expected_result: Required to exclude a superimposed infective trigger; result pending on admission
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A treatable concurrent infection could be missed and immunosuppression given inappropriately.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Distinguishes/identifies an infective trigger; cannot be obtained in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Abdominal X-ray / CT for colonic dilatation (toxic megacolon)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Plain abdominal radiograph (and CT if indicated)
investigation_details.expected_result: Assesses transverse colon diameter for toxic megacolon; not obtainable remotely
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Toxic megacolon and impending perforation could be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Imaging is the definitive test for colonic dilatation and cannot be performed in telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: More than 6 bloody stools/day with tachycardia, fever and clinical anaemia defines severe disease requiring emergency admission.
Ongoing blood/fluid loss with inability to rehydrate orally and postural near-syncope indicates impending hypovolaemic shock.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency hospital assessment with venous bloods (FBC, CRP, U&E, lactate, group and hold)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies anaemia, inflammation and electrolyte/volume status to guide resuscitation and steroid therapy.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Plain abdominal radiograph for toxic megacolon
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Detects colonic dilatation; a normal film does not remove the need for serial monitoring.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Stool C. difficile toxin/PCR and culture
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Identifies a superimposed infective trigger before escalating immunosuppression.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Home/telehealth attempt to measure or numerically quantify blood pressure
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The AI Doctor must not delay escalation to obtain a home BP; the reported near-syncope already mandates emergency transfer.
